---
title: Call Centre
---

Metrics in this section are calculated from call centre activity and topic data.

Call centre metrics are useful for understanding support demand, service
responsiveness, caller wait times, average call duration, and common reasons for
contact. The source data is weekly, so these metrics are typically reported at a
weekly level or aggregated across weeks.

The relevant call centre tables are:

- [`call_centre.weekly_activity_report`](../catalog/call_centre.qmd#weekly_activity_report) - weekly call volume and service-level summary
- [`call_centre.weekly_topic_dump`](../catalog/call_centre.qmd#weekly_topic_dump) - individual call records with topic classifications

`date_range_start` and `date_range_end` are stored as text, so date-range filters
cast them to `DATE` before comparing to date literals.

## Activity Metrics

Metrics in this section describe call centre operations, sourced from
`call_centre.weekly_activity_report`.

{{< include call_centre/calls-accepted.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/calls-answered.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/average-call-length.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/average-delay.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/abandonment-rate.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/answer-rate.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/service-level.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/average-handle-time.qmd >}}

## Topic Metrics

Metrics in this section describe why people contact the call centre, sourced from
`call_centre.weekly_topic_dump`. Because a single call can generate multiple
topic rows, these metrics count topic records rather than distinct calls. Clean
`statobject` before grouping, since it often carries leading, trailing, or
repeated whitespace.

{{< include call_centre/call-topic-share.qmd >}}

{{< include call_centre/top-10-call-topics.qmd >}}
